questions.prompt: Which sequence best describes the pain pathway (from stimulus to conscious awareness)?

choices.A: Nociceptor activation → sensory (afferent) neuron → spinal cord (dorsal horn) → ascending tract → thalamus/cortex
choices.B: Motor neuron activation → spinal cord (ventral horn) → peripheral nerve → nociceptor → cortex
choices.C: Thalamus activation → spinal cord → nociceptor → afferent neuron → cortex
choices.D: Peripheral nerve activation → motor neuron → spinal cord → nociceptor → cortex

questions.answer: Nociceptor activation → sensory (afferent) neuron → spinal cord (dorsal horn) → ascending tract → thalamus/cortex
questions.why: Pain begins at nociceptors, travels via afferent neurons into the spinal cord, then ascends to higher brain centers for perception.
questions.trap: Choices that start with motor pathways or reverse the order confuse sensory vs. motor routes and CNS processing order.
questions.hook: Pain = nociceptor → afferent → spinal cord → up → brain.

questions.prompt: Which list contains the five basic tastes?
questions.why: The widely accepted five basic tastes are sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami (savory).
questions.trap: ‘Spicy/pungent’ are trigeminal sensations, not basic tastes; ‘metallic’ isn’t part of the basic five.
questions.hook: SSSBU = Sweet Sour Salty Bitter Umami.

questions.prompt: Starting at the neuromuscular junction, which sequence best describes skeletal muscle contraction?

choices.A: ACh release → sarcolemma depolarizes → Ca2+ released from SR → actin-myosin cross-bridges → Ca2+ pumped back into SR
choices.B: Ca2+ released from SR → ACh release → cross-bridges → depolarization → Ca2+ pumped into SR
choices.C: ACh release → Ca2+ pumped into SR → depolarization → cross-bridges → Ca2+ released from SR
choices.D: Depolarization → ACh release → Ca2+ pumped into SR → Ca2+ released from SR → cross-bridges

questions.answer: ACh release → sarcolemma depolarizes → Ca2+ released from SR → actin-myosin cross-bridges → Ca2+ pumped back into SR
questions.why: ACh triggers an action potential, which releases Ca2+ to enable cross-bridging; relaxation occurs when Ca2+ is returned to the SR.
questions.trap: Many distractors scramble Ca2+ release vs reuptake or place cross-bridges before Ca2+ release.
questions.hook: ACh → AP → Ca → cross-bridge → Ca back.

questions.prompt: What are the three arches of the foot?
questions.why: The foot’s arches are classically described as two longitudinal arches (medial and lateral) and one transverse arch.
questions.trap: Terms like anterior/posterior or plantar/dorsal are not the standard arch names.

questions.prompt: Striated, voluntary, and attached to bones describes which muscle type?
questions.why: Skeletal muscle is striated and voluntary, producing movement by pulling on bones via tendons.
questions.trap: Cardiac is striated but involuntary; smooth is involuntary and nonstriated. ‘Visceral’ typically refers to smooth, not a separate third category.

questions.prompt: What role does melanin play in the skin?

choices.A: It absorbs UV radiation to help protect deeper tissues and DNA from damage
choices.B: It produces sweat to cool the body
choices.C: It secretes sebum to lubricate hair and skin
choices.D: It forms collagen fibers that provide skin strength

questions.answer: It absorbs UV radiation to help protect deeper tissues and DNA from damage
questions.why: Melanin is a pigment produced by melanocytes that helps shield cells from UV exposure.
questions.trap: Sweat (eccrine glands), sebum (sebaceous glands), and collagen (dermis) are different structures/functions.

questions.prompt: Which option lists five major functions of the skin?

choices.A: Protection, temperature regulation, sensation, vitamin D synthesis, excretion
choices.B: Protection, digestion, respiration, filtration, hormone production
choices.C: Movement, protection, nutrient absorption, memory, oxygen transport
choices.D: Temperature regulation, digestion, circulation, reproduction, hearing

questions.answer: Protection, temperature regulation, sensation, vitamin D synthesis, excretion
questions.why: Key skin functions commonly include barrier protection, thermoregulation, sensory reception, vitamin D production, and limited excretion via sweat.
questions.trap: Many distractors list functions of other systems (digestive, respiratory, nervous, etc.).
questions.hook: Skin = barrier + thermostat + sensors + D + sweat.
